canada_instrument: Strategy plus safety institute funding plus future privacy/online-safety law
comparison_frame: Canada names trust and funds capacity. EU shows hard transparency duties; NIST/Australia show operational controls; G7 shows voluntary model-developer norms; China shows a much harder content-control contrast.

plain_question: Who gets to use my personal data?
comparison_frame: Canada's strategy is directional until privacy and AI accountability rules land. EU/Colorado show rights-and-obligation models; OECD/NIST show baseline principles and risk-management practice.
app_use: Concerned Canadian privacy and accountability topics

plain_question: Will AI keep my child safe?
canada_instrument: Strategy plus future online safety and privacy law
comparison_frame: Canada names children and online harms. EU/Australia give disclosure and guardrail comparators; UNESCO adds child rights and wellbeing; China is a contrast for strict service/content control.

canada_instrument: Strategy plus literacy funding and youth placements
comparison_frame: Canada funds broad AI literacy. Singapore and India are useful for national skills ecosystems; Japan shows business-facing AI competence; OECD gives a trustworthy AI literacy baseline.

canada_instrument: Funded adoption programs plus future trust framework
comparison_frame: Canada's strongest lane is funded adoption. Singapore/Australia/Japan/NIST show the missing practical layer: playbooks, guardrails, lifecycle governance, and risk documentation.

canada_instrument: Strategy support for open-source AI and sovereign infrastructure
comparison_frame: Canada treats open source as resilience and choice. India and France help compare public compute/ecosystem support; Singapore shows governance tools; OECD keeps the trust baseline visible.

plain_question: How does my day-to-day work change?
comparison_frame: Canada is directional for everyday workplace AI. EU, NYC, and Colorado show harder rules for employment/consequential decisions; Australia/NIST show operational workplace guardrails.

plain_question: Will government AI make services better and fairer?
comparison_frame: Canada says government will adopt trusted Canadian AI. US OMB shows agency governance; EU shows hard high-risk duties; UK shows regulator-led oversight; Finland/Sweden show welfare-state public-service comparators.

plain_question: How do I scale my innovation?
canada_instrument: Strategy plus compute, capital, procurement, and partnerships
comparison_frame: Canada's founder story is industrial strategy plus sovereignty. France/Korea/Singapore/India compare national champion and compute strategies; UK shows a lighter regulatory market-access model.

plain_question: Can government become my first serious customer?
comparison_frame: Canada's procurement promise needs governance detail. US OMB and Sweden are public-sector comparators; UK shows sector-regulator logic; EU shows high-risk obligations for public-facing systems.

plain_question: Will value and creative rights stay protected?
canada_instrument: Strategy plus IP supports and creative technology funding
comparison_frame: Canada is stronger on IP/commercialization than on training-data rights. EU gives copyright transparency duties; France gives industrial/IP strategy; UNESCO/OECD add cultural and rights baselines.

plain_question: Will AI improve health care safely?
comparison_frame: Canada funds health as first mission. EU shows high-risk obligations; NIST/OMB show operational risk governance; Norway/UNESCO add public-service and ethics comparators.

plain_question: Will AI make Canada more prosperous for everyone?
canada_instrument: National strategy with headline GDP, jobs, adoption, and mission targets
comparison_frame: Canada's promise mixes productivity, sovereignty, public benefit, and champions. France/Korea compare industrial ambition; Singapore/India compare execution and scale; Sweden keeps public-service and welfare-state outcomes in view.

plain_question: Will AI reflect Canada's languages, cultures, and communities?
canada_instrument: Strategy plus inclusion, culture, Indigenous-led AI, and literacy programs
comparison_frame: Canada is explicit on bilingualism, accessibility, Indigenous-led AI, and culture. UNESCO/OECD provide rights baselines; Nordic strategies help compare language/public-service inclusion.
